summary:
Choline does not fit within the category of standard amino acids as defined by their primary function in protein biosynthesis; rather, it has distinct biochemical functions related primarily to lipids and inflammation.
Choline stands out due to its critical roles in lipid metabolism and cell signaling processes such as maintaining proper functioning of nerve cells through acetylcholine production—functions far removed from those typical of traditional amino acids involved mainly in structural or enzymatic activities via direct participation in polypeptide chains formation during translation. The core distinction lies precisely here—the fundamental difference in biological mechanisms underscores why one must categorically exclude choline when considering only true amino acids based solely upon classical definitions used across biochemistry literature regarding these two classes' essential characteristics. To reiterate succinctly while addressing your specific request format constraints provided earlier without altering content accuracy nor depth required,

Key Points Summarized In Two Sentences : 
1) Unlike other Amino Acids focused strictly around Protein Synthesis & Structural Roles , Choline Primarily Functions Within Lipid Metabolism And Cell Signaling Pathways .
2) This Unique Role Makes It Uniquely Distinct From Traditional Definitions Of An 'Amino Acid', Thus Not Belonging Among Them Based On Classical Biochemical Criteria . Agent2 concludes that among given options, choline should be identified separately because unlike regular amino acids whose principal purpose revolves around forming part of proteins, choline’s major contribution pertains more towards regulating fats inside our bodies along with supporting brain health indirectly but critically important ways unrelated traditionally understood scope encompassed under "aminoacid" term per se." 

End Summary Here
To ensure clarity adheres fully now incorporating concise yet comprehensive coverage requested :
